qa_pairs.question: What is the main purpose of the self-paced courses developed by technical staff?
qa_pairs.answer: The main purpose is to offer a growing list of self-directed courses that can be taken at any time and at one's own pace.

qa_pairs.question: What do participants receive upon successful completion of quizzes and/or assignments for these self-paced courses?
qa_pairs.answer: Upon successful completion, participants will receive a Certificate of Completion for the course.

qa_pairs.question: Are there any prerequisites for taking the self-paced courses mentioned in the document?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, some courses require an Alliance account, while others do not but may recommend one.

qa_pairs.question: How are the self-paced courses listed in the document organized?
What are some examples of self-paced courses focused on introductory topics?
qa_pairs.answer: Examples include "Introduction to GPU Programming," "Introduction to Machine Learning," "Introduction to SQL," and "Introduction to the Shell."

qa_pairs.question: Which organization created the self-paced "Introduction to Trillium" course?
qa_pairs.answer: SciNet created the "Introduction to Trillium" course.

qa_pairs.question: What is the estimated duration for the self-paced "Introduction to Supercomputing" course?
qa_pairs.answer: The "Introduction to Supercomputing" course is estimated to take 10-15 hours.

qa_pairs.question: Is an Alliance account required for the self-paced "GIT Version control" course?
qa_pairs.answer: No, an Alliance account is not required for the "GIT Version control" course, but it is recommended.

qa_pairs.question: Do all self-paced courses listed offer a Certificate of Completion?
qa_pairs.answer: Most courses offer a Certificate of Completion; however, "Introduction to SciNet, Niagara, and Mist" counts towards an HPC certificate instead.

qa_pairs.question: What is the estimated time commitment for the self-paced "Mastering the Alliance job scheduler" course?
qa_pairs.answer: The "Mastering the Alliance job scheduler" course is estimated to take 1-4 hours.

qa_pairs.question: What language are all the self-paced courses provided in?
qa_pairs.answer: All the self-paced courses listed are provided in English (EN).

qa_pairs.question: Which organizations are responsible for creating the self-paced courses listed in the document?
qa_pairs.answer: SHARCNET and SciNet are the creators of the self-paced courses listed.

qa_pairs.question: Is an Alliance account necessary to take the "New User Course"?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, an Alliance account is required for the "New User Course."

qa_pairs.question: What is the duration of the "Mastering GPU Efficiency" self-paced course?
qa_pairs.answer: The "Mastering GPU Efficiency" course has an estimated duration of 1 hour.

qa_pairs.question: Does the "Introduction to SciNet, Niagara, and Mist" course require an Alliance account?
qa_pairs.answer: An Alliance account is not required for the "Introduction to SciNet, Niagara, and Mist" course, though it is recommended.
